title: Types allowed to be deserialized should be restricted
description: During the deserialization process, the state of an object will be reconstructed from the serialized data stream. By allowing unrestricted deserialization of types, the application makes it possible for attackers to use types with dangerous or otherwise sensitive behavior during the deserialization process. What is the potential impact? When an application deserializes untrusted data without proper restrictions, an attacker can craft malicious serialized objects.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects unsafe deserialization patterns including BinaryFormatter, SoapFormatter, NetDataContractSerializer, and JsonSerializerSettings with TypeNameHandling.All/Auto/Objects without SerializationBinder

conditions: TypeNameHandling.All or TypeNameHandling.Auto set without SerializationBinder

never_trigger_on: XmlSerializer or DataContractSerializer with known types
logic.reasoning: Unrestricted deserialization allows attackers to instantiate arbitrary types, leading to remote code execution. BinaryFormatter, SoapFormatter, and NetDataContractSerializer are inherently unsafe. TypeNameHandling.All/Auto/Objects in Json.NET allow type injection unless a SerializationBinder is used to whitelist allowed types.
